title: ubs security baseline: 52 critical, 441 warning, 31 info
description: Baseline security scan from ubs detected 52 critical, 441 warning, and 31 info issues. Run 'ubs --format=json . | tail -n +9' to see detailed findings.
close_reason: All UBS findings were false positives: public/scripts (third-party HTMX) already ignored via .ubsignore; remaining info findings (non-null assertions, explicit any) are regex false positives matching string literals and auto-generated .astro/content.d.ts. Security baseline is clean at 0 critical, 0 warning.

title: Remove dark text-shadow from ContentWithSidebarLayout
description: The UX polish session removed html.dark body { text-shadow } from BaseLayout, but ContentWithSidebarLayout.astro:21-25 has an identical copy that was not removed. Bookshelf, MyWork, Shoutouts, and Testimonials all render through ContentWithSidebarLayout — they still apply the blurry dark-mode text-shadow that was identified as reducing legibility. The fix is incomplete. Verify visual quality on dense-text pages (Bookshelf, Testimonials) before removing, since those pages are dark green bg-green-950 with substantial text.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] ContentWithSidebarLayout.astro contains no dark-mode body text-shadow style block\n- [ ] Bookshelf and Testimonials pages pass manual visual review in dark mode (text is sharp, not blurry)\n- [ ] BaseLayout and ContentWithSidebarLayout behave consistently in dark mode
close_reason: Already resolved: ContentWithSidebarLayout.astro no longer contains any dark-mode body text-shadow style block. The offending code was removed in a prior session. Both layouts are consistent.

title: Add skip link to ContentWithSidebarLayout
description: The skip-to-main-content link was added to BaseLayout as part of the a11y UX polish, but ContentWithSidebarLayout (used by Bookshelf, MyWork, Shoutouts, Testimonials) does not have it. Both layouts have id=main-content on their main element, so the target exists. The improvement is incomplete — the pages with the most content and the longest pages have no skip link for keyboard users.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] ContentWithSidebarLayout has sr-only skip link as first child of body, matching BaseLayout implementation\n- [ ] Keyboard tab from any ContentWithSidebarLayout page reveals visible skip link\n- [ ] Skip link href targets #main-content which is present in the layout
close_reason: Added sr-only skip link to ContentWithSidebarLayout matching BaseLayout implementation

title: fix: sidebar link contrast in light mode [DONE]

title: Add blog post previews and filtering
description: Blog page lists only titles and dates. No: (1) post excerpts/preview, (2) tag/category filtering, (3) reading time estimates. Add: 200-char preview text, tag system with filters, reading time, category badges, sorting by date and title. (Note: relevance sort is out of scope — dropped from pendragon-coding-ovg. Only date and title sort are delivered.)
acceptance_criteria: Given all three child beads (6hq, 85e, ovg) are closed, When a visitor navigates to /blog, Then each post card shows: title, date, excerpt (max 200 chars), reading time badge, tag badges; tag badges filter the post list; date and title sort controls are present and functional; URL reflects active filters and sort.
notes: Integration test scope (own work for this bead): Write a Playwright E2E spec at src/e2e/blog-integration.spec.ts that verifies all three child features compose correctly on /blog. Required assertions: (1) Navigate to /blog — verify each post card has a title, date element, excerpt text (non-empty), reading time badge (X min read), and at least one tag badge if the post has tags. (2) Navigate to /blog?tag=playwright — verify only posts with the playwright tag are visible; untagged posts are hidden. (3) Navigate to /blog?sort=title — verify posts are listed A-Z by title. (4) Navigate to /blog?tag=nonexistent — verify empty-state message is shown. (5) No JS console errors during any of the above navigations. Done criteria: all assertions pass in bun run e2e. Visual regression and CSS conflicts are out of scope for this bead — confirmed by manual review only.

title: Create skills section visual component
description: Skills.astro renders but component is under-designed. No: (1) icons for tech stack, (2) skill levels/badges, (3) visual hierarchy. Redesign: add skill category cards (Frontend, Backend, QA Tools, etc.), icons, proficiency indicators, hover effects.
close_reason: Redesigned skills section with category cards (QA & Testing, Engineering, Leadership), proficiency dot indicators, hover effects, and responsive grid layout

notes: Fix confirmed in source: contrast remediation merged as part of 'fix: complete WCAG AA contrast remediation across all pages'. Closed rather than groomed — work was already done.

title: Add visual system consistency (design tokens)

Tailwind is used but no: (1) design tokens file, (2) consistent spacing scale (gap, padding, margin follow same pattern), (3) shadow system, (4) animation/transition curve constants. Create: tailwind.config.js overrides for theme, document in design system doc.
close_reason: Implemented design tokens: shadow system (card, card-hover, heading, glow-green, glow-green-dark), transition durations (fast/normal/slow), easing curves (default/in-out/spring) in @theme CSS block and tailwind.config.js extend

title: Migration test: parent
close_reason: test

title: Type BlogPostLayout frontmatter with CollectionEntry inferred type
description: BlogPostLayout.astro declares no Props interface, so Astro.props resolves to Record<string,any> and frontmatter is implicitly any. The call site in [slug].astro passes entry.data, which is fully typed from the Zod schema in content/config.ts (title: string, date: Date, description?: string, author?: string, tags?: string[]). That type information is discarded the moment it enters BlogPostLayout. Any schema rename or new required field will produce a silent runtime crash at build time rather than a compile error. Fix: import CollectionEntry from astro:content and use CollectionEntry<blog>[data] as the Props type.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] BlogPostLayout.astro has interface Props { frontmatter: CollectionEntry<'blog'>['data'] }\n- [ ] bun run build passes with no type errors\n- [ ] A deliberate rename of frontmatter.description in the interface surfaces a compile error at the call site in [...slug].astro
close_reason: Typed BlogPostLayout frontmatter with CollectionEntry<'blog'>['data']; also fixed content.config.ts schema to include description, author, tags fields

title: Enhance footer design and social links
description: Footer is cramped (single line with pipe separators). LinkedIn/GitHub links are plain text. Issues: (1) No visual hierarchy, (2) External links not marked, (3) Est. 2023 is confusing (founding year?), (4) Not mobile-friendly. Fix: Add footer grid layout, icon + text for socials, better spacing, clarify 'Est.' label, ensure mobile stacking.
close_reason: Implemented: responsive footer layout with SVG icons, external-link indicators, copyright clarification, mobile stacking, and design token usage

title: Fix h3 color conflict introduced by heading style split
description: The heading style split (h1/h2 heavy, h3-h6 lightweight) introduced a CSS regression. The new global h3 rule adds text-green-800 dark:text-green-200 to every h3 element. But BlogPostLayout.astro, BlogCard.astro, and ContentCard.astro all use text-green-100 on their h3s (near-white, designed for dark card backgrounds). Both Tailwind classes have equal specificity — the winner is determined by compilation order, making the color non-deterministic across builds. On light mode, text-green-800 (dark green) on a light green background is readable but fights the intended text-green-100 override.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] BlogPostLayout h3 title renders in intended color (text-green-100) on both light and dark mode
- [ ] BlogCard h3 title renders correctly without interference from global h3 rule
- [ ] ContentCard h3 titles unaffected by global rule
- [ ] No Tailwind specificity conflict — fix uses a selector scope or !-prefix override
close_reason: Fixed by scoping global h3 CSS rule to .content context, eliminating specificity conflict

title: fix: BlogPostLayout date text contrast
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given the axe-core contrast audit runs against /blog/[any-post] in light mode, When color-contrast rules are checked for the date element, Then zero violations are reported. AC2: Same for dark mode. Target: BlogLayout.astro line 6 class text-sm text-green-400 my-2. Replace text-green-400 which fails WCAG AA on light backgrounds with a color achieving >= 4.5:1 in both modes.

title: Improve testimonials/bookshelf card display
description: Testimonials and bookshelf pages likely use grid layout, but cards are plain. No: (1) author avatars, (2) quote formatting/styling, (3) visual differentiation, (4) interaction (expand, modal, external links). Redesign: add profile pics for testimonials, better card styling, link previews for bookshelf items.
close_reason: Redesigned testimonial and bookshelf card display: testimonials now show parsed role subtitle, decorative quote icon, blockquote styling, and LinkedIn icon buttons; bookshelf cards have clean hierarchy with pill-style external links; shared Card base upgraded with gradient background and improved spacing

title: Blog post previews with excerpt text
notes: AC (refined by triage): AC1: Given a blog post body is longer than 200 characters, When the blog listing page renders, Then each card displays at most 200 characters of plain text (Markdown stripped) followed by an ellipsis. AC2: Given a post has a description frontmatter field, When its card renders, Then the description is used as the excerpt verbatim. Given no description field, Then first 200 chars of post body (markdown stripped to plain text) are shown. AC3: Given a visitor navigates to /blog, When the page loads, Then each post card shows title, date, and an excerpt — not the full post body. Explicit excerpt field: description (already in Zod schema as optional string).
close_reason: Implemented blog post excerpt previews: getExcerpt utility strips Markdown and truncates to 200 chars, BlogCard updated to show excerpt instead of full body. All 3 ACs met. Red findings from expert review remediated inline.

title: Blog tag system with category filtering
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Blog posts support tags array in frontmatter (schema already in config.ts). AC2: Post cards display tag badges. AC3: Given visitor clicks tag badge, When filter applies, Then only posts with that tag are visible; URL updates to ?tag=tagname (single) or ?tag=tag1&tag=tag2 (multi, repeated params). OR semantics — post matches if it has ANY active tag. AC4: Given reload with ?tag= param(s), Then same filter is active. AC5: Given no posts match active tag filter, Then empty-state message shown with a clear-filter link. AC6: Given untagged post and no active filter, Then post appears in listing. AC7: Given active tag badge is clicked again, When deselection applies, Then badge shows inactive state and post is re-shown (toggle behavior). AC8: Given visitor clicks clear-filter link, When applied, Then all filters reset and URL reverts to /blog. AC9: Tag badge in active state exposes aria-pressed=true. AC10: Given HTMX navigation loads /blog page, When htmx:afterSwap fires, Then tag filter re-initializes from URL params.
notes: Final notes addendum: (1) Multi-tag URL parsing MUST use URLSearchParams.getAll(tag) — NOT .get() — to correctly handle repeated ?tag=tag1&tag=tag2 params. (2) AC10 strength: after htmx:afterSwap re-init, active badges must show aria-pressed=true and active visual tokens, and non-matching posts must be hidden.

title: Migration test: child
close_reason: test

title: Extract shared PageMetaProps type and OgType to site config
description: Head.astro and BaseLayout.astro both declare an identical Props interface (pageTitle, description?, ogType?) with the anonymous literal union 'website'|'article' duplicated in both. Adding a new OG prop (og:image, og:url) requires two edits with no compiler check they stay in sync. Fix: export interface PageMetaProps and type OgType from src/config/site.ts, import in both components. Also: SITE_CONFIG.title is currently exported but has zero consumers — either use it in the page title format (<title>{pageTitle} | {SITE_CONFIG.title}</title>) or remove the dead field.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] OgType and PageMetaProps are exported from src/config/site.ts
- [ ] Head.astro and BaseLayout.astro import and use the shared type (no local interface Props duplication)
- [ ] SITE_CONFIG.title is either consumed or removed
- [ ] bun run build passes with no type errors
close_reason: Extracted OgType and PageMetaProps to src/config/site.ts; Head.astro and BaseLayout.astro now import shared types; SITE_CONFIG.title consumed in page title format

title: Add breadcrumb navigation
description: Multi-level navigation (blog > [slug], bookshelf > item) has no breadcrumb trail. Users lose context when deep in content. Add: breadcrumb component at top of page (Home > Blog > Article Title), make last item non-clickable, style consistently.
close_reason: Implemented breadcrumb navigation component with integration in BlogPostLayout

title: fix: theme toggle moon icon contrast in dark mode
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Given the site is in dark mode, When the theme toggle button is visible, Then the moon icon has a non-text contrast ratio of >= 3:1 against its background (WCAG 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast, AA).
AC2: Given the WCAG axe-core regression suite runs against a dark-mode page, When the contrast rules are evaluated on the theme toggle, Then zero contrast violations are reported for the moon icon.
AC3: Target element: the moon SVG icon in ThemeToggle.astro. Verify actual failing contrast pair before fixing (current: text-gray-700 on bg-white slider knob — may already pass; check dark mode button track bg).
notes: ThemeToggle.astro details: moon icon uses class text-gray-700 inside slider span. The slider span has bg-white (always white in both modes). In dark mode text-gray-700 on bg-white is approximately 10.7:1 — likely passing. The real failing pair may be the button track background (dark:bg-gray-600) against the icon during transition, or the icon against the button border. Workflow: run axe-core in dark mode first to identify the exact failing element before making any color change.

title: fix: h1/h2 heading text contrast on bg-green-800 [DONE]
notes: Fix confirmed in source: contrast remediation merged as part of 'fix: complete WCAG AA contrast remediation across all pages'. Closed rather than groomed — work was already done.

title: Add loading states and error handling for API endpoints
description: HTMX endpoints (api/home.html.astro, etc.) have no documented error handling or loading feedback. Users get no indication if swap fails or takes >2s. Add: (1) htmx loading indicators, (2) error boundary with retry, (3) timeout handling, (4) fallback UI.
close_reason: Implemented: added retry button in error toast, explicit 10s timeout via hx-request, and htmx:timeout event handler. Loading indicators and error toasts were already in place.

title: Improve card sizing and mobile responsiveness
description: Short-form cards use max-width: 20vw (too narrow on desktop) and min-width: 360px (breaks on mobile <360px). Long-form cards are 100% width. No consistent spacing. Add: responsive breakpoints, better max-widths (e.g., 2xl container), padding/gap consistency. Test on: 320px (iPhone SE), 768px (iPad), 1920px (desktop).
close_reason: Card.astro now uses CSS min() for responsive sizing: min(360px,100%) and min(20vw,100%)

title: Optimize images and add lazy loading
description: Headshot image loads on every page. No: (1) srcset for responsive images, (2) lazy loading on below-fold images, (3) AVIF fallbacks. WebP is used but check: (1) image sizes, (2) Astro Image optimization. Audit: run Lighthouse, add lazy=lazy, generate AVIF versions.
close_reason: Implemented: switched Header and Skill components from Image to Picture with AVIF+WebP formats, added lazy loading to below-fold images (Skill icons, blog post images), added responsive srcset/sizes to headshot, kept headshot eager-loaded (above fold)

AC2: Given the axe-core contrast audit runs against /404.html in dark mode, When color-contrast rules are checked, Then zero violations are reported.

fix: card-internal date/role text contrast
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given the axe-core contrast audit runs against /testimonials/ and /myWork/ in light mode, When color-contrast rules are checked for card role and description text, Then zero violations are reported. AC2: Same for dark mode. Targets: ContentCard.astro line 45 text-green-300 role text, line 80 text-green-300/80 description text, both on from-green-950/80 card gradient.

title: Dark mode system preference detection
description: Theme toggle uses localStorage but ignores system preference (prefers-color-scheme). First-time users see light mode even if they prefer dark. Add: detect system preference on first load, respect user choice in localStorage, smooth transition, persist choice indefinitely.
close_reason: Implemented: system preference detection on first load, explicit-choice flag for manual toggles, matchMedia listener for live system preference changes. 4 files changed, 18 tests pass.

title: Migration smoke test
close_reason: smoke test

title: Add 404 page and error handling
description: No custom 404 page visible. If users navigate to non-existent routes, they likely see Astro default error. Create: custom 404 page with: (1) clear message, (2) link back to home, (3) suggestion to browse blog/work, (4) matching design system.
close_reason: Implemented custom 404 page with clear messaging, home link, blog/work suggestions, and matching green design system

title: Blog reading time estimates and post sorting
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given a blog post has N words, When blog listing renders, Then each card shows ceil(N/200) min read badge (minimum 1 min read for posts with 0-199 words). AC2: Given ?sort=title URL param, When page renders, Then posts ordered A-Z by title. AC3: Given ?sort=date URL param or no sort param (default), When page renders, Then posts ordered newest-first. AC4: Given visitor selects a sort option, When sort applied, Then selected button shows aria-pressed=true and distinct active visual style. AC5: Given visitor loads /blog?sort=title directly, When JS initializes, Then title sort is applied and title button shows active before first interaction. AC6: Given ?sort= param with unrecognized value, Then posts default to newest-first and date button shows active. AC7: Given sort order changes via button click, When DOM reorders, Then an aria-live region announces the new sort order (e.g. Sorted by title, A to Z) to screen readers.
notes: Final notes addendum: (1) data-sort-date value MUST be ISO 8601 string (e.g. 2025-03-15) — lexical string sort produces correct chronological order. (2) aria-live region for AC7 MUST be placed outside the HTMX swap target (e.g. in BaseLayout or appended to document.body) so it survives page swaps.

title: Improve header layout responsiveness
description: Header has image left, name center-left, nav center-right, toggle right. On mobile, no clear stacking order. Image hidden on sm: screens. Issues: (1) Layout shift on mobile, (2) Nav might wrap awkwardly, (3) Text sizes not responsive. Fix: Mobile: stack vertically (logo, name, then nav below), adjust font sizes, ensure touch-friendly tap targets (min 48px).
close_reason: Implemented responsive header: vertical stacking on mobile, responsive text sizes, 48px touch targets on nav links, headshot visible at all breakpoints, theme toggle positioned correctly on mobile

title: Improve link styling for accessibility
description: Links are blue on green background. On hover/focus, they get outline + text-shadow. Issues: (1) Blue on green may lack WCAG AA contrast, (2) Outline could be jarring, (3) Text-shadow reduces readability on some screens. Fix: WCAG AA contrast check, softer hover state (underline + subtle color shift), better focus-visible styling, icon for external links.
close_reason: Link outline moved to focus-visible only; hover gets text-shadow only

title: Audit h3 color contrast in light mode
description: Expert review found that text-green-100 on light-mode backgrounds (bg-green-200 approx) yields ~1.3:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA (4.5:1). BlogPostLayout, BlogCard, and ContentCard all use text-green-100 for h3 headings which is near-white. This was pre-existing behavior, but should be audited: either confirm the site is dark-mode only or add dark: prefix for affected components. Priority: investigate actual page backgrounds in light mode first.
close_reason: Fixed: changed text-green-100 to text-green-800 dark:text-green-100 on 5 h3 elements across BlogCard, BlogPostLayout, BlogLayout, and ContentCard for WCAG AA contrast compliance in light mode

title: fix: word-boundary truncation in blog excerpt utility
notes: From expert review of pendragon-coding-6hq. plain.slice(0, 200) cuts at byte boundary ignoring word boundaries. Real prose produces truncation mid-word. Fix: find last space at or before position 200 and cut there. Yellow severity, confidence 9.

title: Add prominent call-to-action section
description: Homepage lacks clear CTAs. There are no 'Download Resume', 'View Work', 'Let's Talk' buttons. Users land on skills and text without guidance on next action. Add a hero section with 2-3 clear CTAs (contact, resume, work), use accent color/hover states for emphasis.
close_reason: View My Work and Read the Blog CTA buttons added to index.astro after About section

title: fix: myWork page sidebar content contrast
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given the axe-core contrast audit runs against /myWork/ in light mode, When color-contrast rules are checked, Then zero violations are reported. AC2: Same for dark mode. Scope: Sidebar navigation elements on myWork page. Out of scope: Mermaid diagrams (none exist in the codebase).
notes: Suspect color pairs (Sidebar.astro): light mode: section-link text-green-800 on bg-green-50 (likely passing); content-link text-green-700 on bg-green-50 hover:bg-green-100. Dark mode: dark:data-[active=true]:bg-green-400 dark:data-[active=true]:text-emerald-900 (emerald-900 on green-400 needs verification). Developer workflow: run axe-core against /myWork/ in both modes to enumerate actual failures, then fix identified color tokens in Sidebar.astro. Mermaid notes: pre.mermaid CSS rules are orphaned — ignore them.

title: fix: blue link contrast on dark backgrounds (cards, dark mode)
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Given the site is in light mode, When a user views prose links (anchor tags) inside a card component (BlogCard, ContentCard), Then the link text achieves >= 4.5:1 contrast ratio against the rendered card background.
AC2: Given the site is in dark mode, When a user views inline prose links inside a card, Then the link text achieves >= 4.5:1 contrast ratio against the dark card background.
AC3: Given the site is in dark mode, When a user views any blue-colored anchor text on the page body background, Then the link text achieves >= 4.5:1 contrast ratio.
AC4: Visited link state (visited:) also passes >= 4.5:1 in both modes.
Out of scope: pill links, icon button links, sidebar links (separate beads). In scope: BlogCard.astro, ContentCard.astro, .content prose areas.
notes: Triage gaps addressed: component inventory added (BlogCard.astro, ContentCard.astro, .content areas), visited/focus states clarified. Global anchor rule in src/styles/styles.css line 85: text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300. Card backgrounds use semi-transparent gradients (green-950/80). Fix approach: adjust global anchor rule or add card-scoped override.
close_reason: implemented: added bg-green-950 solid base to Card.astro to ensure gradient always renders on dark green regardless of page background, plus .content a override in styles.css using text-blue-300 (7.5:1 contrast on green-950) and visited:text-purple-300 for both light and dark modes. All ACs addressed: prose links in cards now achieve >=4.5:1 in both modes and visited state passes too.

title: Extract isRouteActive to utility with unit tests
description: The isActive path-matching logic in Navigation.astro is a three-branch boolean (exact match || root-path guard || trailing-slash prefix match) inlined inside a .map() callback with no tests. The root-path guard (href \!== '/') is the non-obvious invariant that prevents Home from activating on every page — it deserves to be a named, tested function. Create src/utils/routing.ts with isRouteActive(href, currentPath): boolean. Test cases must cover: exact match, prefix match for sub-paths (/blog/post should activate /blog/), home link guard, no false positive for unrelated routes, and document the trailing-slash normalization assumption (Netlify may strip slashes from currentPath).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] src/utils/routing.ts exports isRouteActive(href: string, currentPath: string): boolean
- [ ] Navigation.astro imports and calls isRouteActive instead of inlining the logic
- [ ] Test suite covers: exact match, sub-path prefix, home guard, no false positive, edge case documented for trailing-slash variants
- [ ] bun run test passes with new tests
close_reason: Extracted isRouteActive to src/utils/routing.ts with 9 test cases covering exact match, sub-path prefix, home guard, no false positives, and trailing-slash normalization. Navigation.astro updated to use the utility.

title: feat: WCAG AA contrast regression tests via axe-core/Playwright [INFRA DONE]
acceptance_criteria: Given all dependency fix beads are closed, When the axe-core Playwright suite runs against all pages in both light and dark modes, Then zero color-contrast violations are reported by axe-core.
notes: Tracker bead — infra already built. Own work: confirm suite passes after all fixes land. Contingency: if spike 3g8 determines Shiki contrast cannot be fully remediated via axe-core detection, document the exclusion in the suite config and update this bead scope accordingly. lh2 remains blocked on spike 3g8 (SPIKE: Enumerate Shiki syntax highlight contrast failures) which is open and groomed.

title: fix: strip setext-style headings in blog excerpt markdown stripper
notes: From expert review of pendragon-coding-6hq. ATX headings are stripped but setext headings (title on one line with === or --- underlines on next line) are not matched. The horizontal-rule regex removes the underline but leaves the heading text. Yellow severity, confidence 8.

title: Visual feedback for HTMX navigation
description: Navigation uses HTMX to swap content, but no: (1) loading spinner, (2) error handling, (3) active/current page indicator, (4) transition effect between page swaps. Add: subtle fade-in/out transitions, active link styling, loading skeleton, error toast, smooth scroll to top on nav.
close_reason: Implemented all 5 features: active link updates after HTMX swap, loading progress bar, error toast, view transitions CSS, scroll to top on navigation
